lesson.menu_desc: Minutes vocabulary + reported speech backshift -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- Meeting Minutes: Recording Decisions & Actions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eduarda | Aula 18 -- Meeting Minutes

listenings.text: Hi Eduarda, a quick note before you finalise the minutes from this morning. Remember, minutes are a record, written after the meeting, so everything shifts to the past. If the partner said the deal is on track, you write he said the deal was on track. If she said we will close on Friday, you write she said we would close on Friday. List the attendees at the top, and note the apologies for those absent. For every decision, record it as a resolution, and give each action an owner. One item was postponed, so mark it as tabled. Send me the draft before you circulate it.

Hi team, a reminder on how we take minutes here. Minutes are the official record of a meeting, so we report in the past. When someone says I agree, we write she said she agreed. When someone says we can proceed, we write he said they could proceed. Always open with the attendees and the apologies. Confirm there was a quorum before any vote. Record each decision as a resolution, with an owner and a deadline. If an item is not resolved, it is tabled for the next meeting. Then the chair adjourns, and we share the minutes.

extra_audio.text: Eduarda listed the attendees and noted the apologies at the top. She confirmed there was a quorum. She recorded each decision as a resolution with an owner. She marked the unresolved item as tabled. She wrote that the chair adjourned the meeting.
